classifier_reason: Bitcoin is a layer-1 network with no central issuer; canonical pages and bitcoin.org show no stable responsible entity, so only light confirmation is warranted.
Ethereum is a layer-1 network with no central issuer; official Ethereum pages and the Ethereum Foundation site show a stable stewarding foundation even though no company runs the protocol.
USDT is a stablecoin issued by Tether; official Tether pages describe USDt as the flagship product and an issued token.
XRP is the native token of the XRP Ledger, and Ripple's official pages say Ripple is a technology company and one of many developers rather than the controller of XRP.

BNB originated at Binance and is still described by Binance materials as created by Binance, but the BNB Chain ecosystem is now community-driven, so the responsible entity is less direct than a standard issuer.
USDC is a regulated stablecoin issued by Circle, so the responsible issuer is clear and worth a full entity mapping.
Solana is a layer-1 network with an obvious stewarding foundation referenced directly on the official site.
TRON is a layer-1 network with a clear DAO stewardship structure, so a full project-to-entity mapping is justified.
Dogecoin is a community token, but the Dogecoin Foundation is an active steward so the token deserves a full check for responsible entities.
Hyperliquid is a newer protocol and DEX ecosystem with a foundation-backed stewardship signal, so a full review is warranted.
LEO is the utility token for Bitfinex and the iFinex ecosystem, with clear issuer/operator signals in official support materials.
Cardano is a layer-1 network with a clear stewardship foundation and official ecosystem pages for the project and foundation.
Bitcoin Cash is a decentralized forked network with no single controlling issuer, so only light confirmation is justified.
Chainlink is a protocol stack with an explicit foundation stewardship signal in official Chainlink pages.
Monero is an openly decentralized community currency with no central company or foundation issuer, so light confirmation is sufficient.
USDe is a stablecoin issued by Ethena and documented by Ethena's official terms as being issued by Ethena GmbH.
Canton is a newer layer-1 network with a foundation stewardship signal on the official site, so full mapping is warranted.
DAI is a decentralized stablecoin managed by Maker Protocol and MakerDAO rather than a single company issuer, so the DAO wrapper is the best available mapping.
Zcash has clear stewardship and development entities in its official ecosystem pages, so a full mapping is justified.
Stellar is an open network whose official materials name the Stellar Development Foundation as steward, so full mapping is warranted.
USD1 is a stablecoin launched by World Liberty Financial and legally issued by BitGo Trust Company, so full entity mapping is required.
Litecoin is a decentralized L1 whose official site is organized around the Litecoin Foundation, so full mapping is appropriate.
PYUSD is PayPal's stablecoin and the official PayPal/Paxos pages identify Paxos as the issuer, so a full mapping is warranted.
Avalanche's official about and foundation pages say the Avalanche Foundation supports the network's long-term growth and resilience, so a full entity mapping is warranted.
Hedera Foundation's official site says it supports adoption of Hedera and the public network is governed by major organizations, so full entity mapping is warranted.
Sui Foundation is the official steward named on Sui's about page, so a full mapping is warranted.
Bittensor's official docs describe the network as open source and community owned, with governance moving away from the Opentensor Foundation, so no stable entity is clear.
Shib.io and SHIB DAO docs explicitly identify the Shib Doggy DAO Foundation as the ecosystem steward, so full mapping is warranted.
MemeCore's official homepage footer names Anteater Labs Pte. Ltd., so the project company is explicit and full mapping is warranted.
TON Foundation is the official non-profit steward for Toncoin and the TON ecosystem, so full mapping is warranted.
Cronos is a live layer-1 blockchain token with strong official entity signals, so full project and responsible-entity search is warranted.
WLFI is an official governance token for an active branded platform with clear company and website signals, so full entity mapping is warranted.
XAUT is a gold-backed token with an explicit issuer and redemption structure, so full entity mapping is warranted.
PAXG is a gold-backed token with a regulated issuer and custodian, so full entity mapping is warranted.
Mantle is a live Ethereum L2 with official site, terms, and treasury pages naming Mantle as the provider, so full entity mapping is warranted.
DOT is Polkadot's native governance asset and the project has an explicit off-chain governance wrapper, so full entity mapping is warranted.
USDG is a regulated USD-backed stablecoin with a clear issuer, so full entity mapping is warranted.
UNI is the governance token for the Uniswap protocol, which has both an operating company and an independent foundation stewarding the ecosystem, so full mapping is warranted.
SKY is a protocol governance token with a named legal operator for the voting portal, but the underlying governance remains decentralized, so full mapping with moderate confidence is warranted.
OKB is the official utility token of the OKX ecosystem and is clearly tied to an exchange operator, so full entity mapping is warranted.
NEAR is a layer-1 protocol token with an explicit foundation steward, so full entity mapping is warranted.
Pi is a live L1 network token with an explicit foundation steward but broader company ambiguity, so full mapping is warranted.
ASTER is the governance/utility token for a decentralized exchange with clear official operator terms but an unclear legal wrapper, so full mapping is warranted.
USDD is a USD-pegged stablecoin with an explicit issuer/custodian in the official docs, so full entity mapping is warranted.
Pepe is a meme/community token with no obvious issuer or operator, so only a light check is warranted.

RLUSD is a dollar-backed stablecoin with an explicit issuer and redemption structure, so full entity mapping is warranted.
ICP is a live layer-1 protocol token with a clear foundation steward, so full entity mapping is warranted.
BGB is the native platform token of Bitget and Bitget Wallet, so full entity mapping to the exchange operator is warranted.

Official Trump is a branded meme token with the website terms naming Fight Fight Fight LLC and the site tokenomics naming Fight Fight Fight LLC and CIC Digital LLC as the controlling companies, so full entity search is warranted.
